zh.lifeSummary: 當Constance de Tosny於1215年出生在威爾士拉德諾斯爾潘斯卡斯特爾時，她的父親Ralph de Tosny25歲，母親Petronilla de Lacy20歲。她和Fulk FitzWarin IV至少有2個兒子和2個女兒。她去世於1266年2月11日在英國格盧斯特斯爾阿爾維斯頓享年51歲，埋葬在英國格盧斯特斯爾阿爾維斯頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Constance de Tosny于1215年出生在威尔士拉德諾斯爾潘斯卡斯特爾时，她的父亲Ralph de Tosny25岁，母亲Petronilla de Lacy20岁。她和Fulk FitzWarin IV至少有2个儿子和2个女儿。她去世于1266年2月11日在英国格盧斯特斯爾阿爾維斯頓享年51岁，埋葬在英国格盧斯特斯爾阿爾維斯頓。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Tosny
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Jean Pierre.
Americanized form of any of various surnames ultimately derived from the Latin personal name Antonius (see Anthony ), for example Greek Antoniou and Italian Toni .
French: from the personal name Tony, a pet form of Antonin (from Latin Antoninus, a derivative of Antonius; see Anthony ).

variants.name: Anthony
meanings: English and West Indian (mainly Antigua and Barbuda, Saint Lucia, and Trinidad and Tobago, also Dutch Caribbean): from the personal name Anthony, Latin Antonius, which, with its variants and cognates, is one of the commonest personal names in Europe. Spellings with -h-, which first appear in English in the 16th century and in French (as Anthoine) at about the same time, are due to the erroneous belief that the name derives from Greek anthos ‘flower’. The popularity of the personal name in Christendom is largely due to the cult of the Egyptian hermit Saint Anthony ( AD 251–356), who in his old age gathered a community of hermits around him, and for that reason is regarded by some as the founder of monasticism. It was further increased by the fame of Saint Anthony of Padua (1195–1231), who long enjoyed a great popular cult and who is believed to help people find lost things. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates and derivatives (patronymics) from other languages, for example Greek patronymic Antoniades , Italian Antoni , Polish Antoniewicz , Croatian and Serbian Antonović (see Antonovich ) and Antunović; see also below. The name Anthony is also found among Christians in southern India, but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Antony .

German, Flemish, and French (mainly Alsace): Latinized (humanistic) patronymic from local equivalents of the Latin personal name Antonius, from its genitive form Antoni(i). In North America, this surname is also an altered form of the German, Dutch, French, and Slovak cognates Antoni 1 and Antony 2.
History: John Anthony of Hampstead, Middlesex, England (now part of north London) migrated to Boston, MA, in 1634. By 1640 he had moved to Providence, RI, where his descendants are still established.

nameMeanings.name: Constance
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval form of the Late Latin name Constantia, which is either a feminine derivative of Constans ( see Constant ) or an abstract noun meaning ‘constancy’. This was a popular name among the Normans, and was borne by, amongst others, the formidable Constance of Sicily ( 1158–98 ), wife of the Emperor Henry VI .

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Constance de Tosny was born in 1215, in Painscastle, Radnorshire, Wales, her father, Ralph de Tosny, was 25 and her mother, Petronilla de Lacy, was 20. She had at least 2 sons and 2 daughters with Fulk FitzWarin IV. She died on 11 February 1266, in Alveston, Gloucestershire, England, at the age of 51, and was buried in Alveston, Gloucestershire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Constance de Tosny est née en 1215, à Painscastle, Radnorshire, Galles, son père, Ralph de Tosny, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Petronilla de Lacy, avait 20 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles avec Fulk FitzWarin IV. Elle est décédée le 11 février 1266, à Alveston, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 51 ans, et a été enterrée à Alveston, Gloucestershire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Constance de Tosny 1215 in Painscastle, Radnorshire, Wales geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Ralph de Tosny 25 und ihre Mutter Petronilla de Lacy 20. Sie hatte mit Fulk FitzWarin IV mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 11. Februar 1266 im Alter von 51 Jahren in Alveston, Gloucestershire, England und wurde in Alveston, Gloucestershire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Constance de Tosny è nata il nel 1215 nel Painscastle, Radnorshire, Galles, sua padre, Ralph de Tosny, aveva 25 anni e sua madre, Petronilla de Lacy, aveva 20 anni. Lei avuto almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie con Fulk FitzWarin IV. Morì il 11 febbraio 1266, nel Alveston, Gloucestershire, England, all'età di 51 anni, e fu sepolta a Alveston, Gloucestershire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Constance de Tosnyが1215年にウェールズラドノーシュアイアペインスカストルで生まれた時，父Ralph de Tosnyは25歳，母Petronilla de Lacyは20歳であった。彼女はFulk FitzWarin IVとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1266年2月11日にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアアルベストンで51歳で死去し，イギリスグロウセステルシュアイアアルベストンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Constance de Tosny(이)가 1215년 웨일스 라드노르실르 파인스카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Ralph de Tosny(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Petronilla de Lacy(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 Fulk FitzWarin IV(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1266년 2월 11일 영국 그루스스터실르 앨베스턴에서 51세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 그루스스터실르 앨베스턴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Constance de Tosny nasceu em 1215, em Painscastle, Radnorshire, Gales, seu pai, Ralph de Tosny, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Petronilla de Lacy, tinha 20 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas com Fulk FitzWarin IV. Ela faleceu em 11 de fevereiro de 1266, em Alveston, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, com 51 anos, e foi sepultada em Alveston, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Constance de Tosny родилась в 1215 г. в местности пеинскастл, радноршир, Уэльс, её отцу по имени Ralph de Tosny было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Petronilla de Lacy, 20. У неё было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери от Fulk FitzWarin IV. Она умерла 11 февраля 1266 г. в местности алвестон, глоусестиршир, Англия в возрасте 51 год и была похоронена в местности алвестон, глоусестиршир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Constance de Tosny nació en 1215, en Painscastle, Radnorshire, Gales, su papá, Ralph de Tosny, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Petronilla de Lacy, tenía 20 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas con Fulk FitzWarin IV. Murió el 11 de febrero de 1266, en Alveston, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 51 años, y fue sepultada en Alveston, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra.
